rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 26, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. SCHEIDING V. DINWIDDIE CONSTRUCTION CO. (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 64 IS CONTROLLING. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM JOHN CURTSINGER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 26, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 8, 2009 PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: OLIVER MCFARLINE PHILLIPS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 26, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: VANTAGE INTERACTIVE, LLC, A NEVADA LIMITED VS. JOEL HOUSEHOLTER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 26, 2008, line 4, CROSS-DEFENDANT VANTAGE INTERACTIVE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CONGRESS INTENDED TO PREEMPT THE FIELD OF REGULATING E-MAIL MESSAGES BY ADOPTING THE CAN SPAM ACT. CONGRESS DID LEAVE AN EXCEPTION FOR STATE STATUTES THAT REGULATE OR PROHIBIT FALSITY. AT THIS TIME, THERE IS NO CALIFORNIA DECISION INTERPRETING THE CARVE OUT. FEDERAL COURTS HAVE FOUND THAT B&P SECTION 17529.5 IS PREEMPTED AND THAT IS CONSISTENT WITH THE INTENT OF CONGRESS. COMMON LAW FRAUD CLAIMS ARE NOT PREEMPTED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: BERNARD BOLTON et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 26, 2008, line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD THE STATUTORY BASIS FOR DIRECT LIABILITY OR FACTS ESTABLISHING A BASIS FOR VICARIOUS LIABILITY. MUNOZ V. CITY OF UNION (2004) 120 CAL.APP.4TH 1077, 1111-1115 CONTEMPLATES THAT THE NEGLIGENT EMPLOYEE BE IDENTIFIED, IF NOT NAMED, IN THE ACTION WHERE PLAINTIFF RELIES ON RESPONDEAT SUPERIOR. =(302/PJM/PB)
